interpretation: The curve is broadly upward-sloping but exhibits a hump, with the 2Y yield (11.95%) exceeding the 5Y (10.82%) and 10Y (11.15%), while the 15Y (12.68%) peaks highest. The 2Y anomaly likely reflects acute banking sector liquidity stress and aggressive BB tightening expectations, as the repo rate at 10% anchors the short end. Persistent inflation (hovering ~10%) drives elevated term premia. The 364D dip below 182D signals near-term uncertainty around BB monetary policy trajectory.
implications: The 2Y bulge suggests banks face severe short-to-medium-term funding pressure, likely bidding up 2Y yields amid tight CRR constraints and deposit competition
The 130bps 10Y-91D spread indicates modest term premium, reflecting market expectations that BB may maintain restrictive policy longer to combat sticky inflation
Segmented demand across tenors — AD banks concentrated in shorter maturities while provident/insurance funds anchor the long end — is distorting the curve shape
